title: "Print board" feature disappeared
excerpt:

posts.author: brandonha (Brandon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

I can’t see “Print board” in the board’s menu.

From Google it used to look like this:

image512×531 45.7 KB

My menu currently looks like this:

And if I try and print via my browser’s print dialogue, my print preview says “To print the board please click on the print button in the board’s menu”. It looks like this:

Is anyone else experiencing this printing issue?

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
DelBocaVista (Marty Fishdog)
posts.cookedHtml: Print board is no longer an option on our end either.

posts.author: Henk-jan (Henk-Jan)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here. No option anymore on our end

posts.author: curtfelch1 (Curtis Felch)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here. So weird. No documentation on where it went, or why. What kind of company just drops that and doesn’t let you know a workaround?

posts.author: Joeymoto96 (Joey Rogers)
posts.cookedHtml: The print feature has always been annoying for Monday. You couldn’t print anything that was useful. And now that its gone its even more annoying!! It would be nice to be able to print individual items or the board as a whole in a useable list form.

posts.author: Melodie (/)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,

Is there any information about the disapearance of the “print board” feature ?

Thank you,

posts.author: RachelShuck (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml: The feature is still gone but at least you can now print the webpage without the annoying error

posts.author: TheOrganized (Bob Richard)
posts.cookedHtml: @RachelShuck How did you do this? I got it to work only once, miraculously, but usually the print preview screen says “Failed to print board” on my page. I am right clicking or using ctrl-P.

posts.author: RachelShuck (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bob, it’s stopped working for me again too

posts.author: taylor_tls (Taylor McBride)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Is there any update on this? Or a workaround that anyone has tried?

I used to use the Print feature everyday. Now I have to export boards to excel and reformat everything, which takes forever and I lose a lot of information from the formula columns.

Let me know Thank you!

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @brandonha ,

you can use the app DocExport PDF Generator to print out your boards.

The app uses Word templates with placeholders. So you can specify which columns you want to export and create your own design. You can also use tables inside the Word template to reproduce the look of monday. Here you can see how to get the color coding for status values and groups in your report:
https://www.docexport.com/help/template/colors/

It’s also possible to create a monday automation so that the report is automatically send via email every day / week / month.
In the DocExport Template Gallery you can find examples you can then modify to fit your needs.

DocExport works with formulas, mirror-columns, images, QR-Codes, Charts and Columns,mn Summaries.

DocExport_ProjectReportwithColor1875×1406 163 KB

cc @DelBocaVista @Henk-jan @curtfelch1 @Joeymoto96 @Melodie @RachelShuck @TheOrganized @taylor_tls
